title: sorbus aucuparia lanuginosa
intro: Sorbus aucuparia lanuginosa, commonly known as the downy rowan tree, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe and Western Asia. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The bark and leaves of the tree contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Sorbus aucuparia lanuginosa is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other minor health complaints.
